name: Sugar Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 1/8 cup powdered sugar, sifted if it is lump
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the butter and sugars together, then add the oil and mix well.
2. Add the eggs and vanilla, then mix well.
3. Sift the flour, baking soda, cream of tartar, and salt together, then add to the mixture and mix well.
4. Refrigerate overnight, or until the dough is firm. If you want to speed up this process, place the dough in the freezer, but keep an eye on it.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. Drop dough by about 3 ml amounts onto an ungreased cookie sheet. Dip a glass into the dough and wipe off excess. Then dip the glass in sugar and flatten a cookie. Repeat the dip-in-sugar, flatten-cookie sequence until all are done. The cookies won't spread during baking, so you can easily judge how far apart to place them (you want room to cook but not to grow). If the dough becomes too soft to flatten easily, refrigerate it between batches.
7. Bake for about 5-6 minutes, or until the edges are just beginning to turn golden brown. Remove from the cookie sheet and place on waxed paper or foil to cool.
